kicker: MT LEVEL II // WRITING THE PROCEDURE

beats.narration: Everything in this course funnels into one document: the written MT procedure. The bar it clears? A tech who's never seen the part matches your result.

payload.title: THE STRANGER TEST
payload.sub: A procedure another technician can run and match your result.

beats.narration: So it follows a required skeleton: foreword and references, apparatus and settings, the product and test area, test conditions, application steps, then recording.

payload.heading: The required skeleton
payload.note: FOREWORD · APPARATUS · PRODUCT · CONDITIONS · STEPS · RECORDS

beats.narration: Two rules carry it. Every setting gets a tolerance. Every action gets one numbered step. Anything vague is a hole the stranger falls through.
Recording closes it out. State the convention: linear means length at least three times width. Log location and settings, then sign with level and date.
If a step lives only in someone's head, one busy Friday erases it. Write it tight, validate it, then follow it exactly.

payload.sub: If it lives only in your head, it isn't the process.
